Write a function `solve_constraints(n: int, constraints: list[tuple[str, int, int]]) -> list[int]`

You are assigning values 1..n to n slots (0-indexed). Each slot gets a unique value.
Constraints are triples of (type, i, j) where i and j are slot indices:
  ('lt', i, j) means slot[i] < slot[j]
  ('diff', i, j) means |slot[i] - slot[j]| >= 2
  ('adj', i, j) means |slot[i] - slot[j]| == 1

Return any valid assignment as a list of n integers, or an empty list if impossible.

Examples:
  solve_constraints(3, [('lt', 0, 1), ('lt', 1, 2)]) -> [1, 2, 3]
  solve_constraints(3, [('lt', 0, 1), ('lt', 1, 2), ('lt', 2, 0)]) -> []
